entry.title: FRUN 5.0 SP00 : Introducing the New Maintenance Certificate Management UI: Simplified & Streamlined
entry.content:
The Maintenance Certificate Management application empowers organizations to efficiently manage maintenance certificates for their SAP systems. It offers a comprehensive overview of managed systems crucial for license distribution.

With this application, users can:

  • Monitor License Status: View the status of installed and available product licenses and maintenance certificates.
  • Download Licenses: Access and download licenses for managed systems effortlessly.
  • Automate License Distribution: Set up automated distribution for Application Server ABAP and Java systems.
  • Detailed Insights: Drill down into license and maintenance certificate details, including data from SAP for Me and managed systems.

With the latest release of Focused Run 5.0 SP00, the Maintenance Certificate Management UI has undergone a major transformation. This upgrade delivers enhanced performance and a unified user experience, making it more intuitive and efficient than ever before.

Stay tuned as I guide you through the exciting new features and how to make the most of this improved interface! 

Enhanced User Interface: From Limited Filters to Personalized Insights

In the earlier Maintenance Certificate Management application built on ABAP Web Dynpro, users could filter data during the initial load only by Customer ID.

With the new SAPUI5-based interface, the filtering capabilities have been significantly enhanced. Users can now filter data during the initial load using not just Customer ID but also additional criteria such as:

  • Extended System ID
  • System Type
  • IT Admin Role
  • Customer Network

This expanded filtering functionality, powered by scope selection, enables users to create more personalized views of their data. This approach aligns with the personalized user experience seen across other use cases in FRUN, providing a consistent and tailored experience for better decision-making.

 

 

 

 

Simplified Data Display and Enhanced Control for Automatic Distribution

After applying the desired filters, the relevant managed systems are displayed in a table format. While the table columns remain largely consistent with the previous application, an important enhancement has been introduced:

  • New Column for Automatic Distribution: A dedicated column now allows users to activate or deactivate automatic license distribution directly.

Additionally, the old button for managing Automatic Distribution has been replaced with a single-click toggle button, streamlining the process for greater ease of use.

With this intuitive design update, users can now manage automatic distribution with minimal effort, making the application more efficient and user-friendly.

 

            

 

 

Streamlined Search, Download, and Action Options

The new application introduces a quick search feature, allowing users to effortlessly locate systems using criteria such as Extended SID, Installation Number, or System Number from the list of managed systems.

Downloading Licenses is a straightforward process:

  1. Select the systems.
  2. Click the Download Licenses button to retrieve the licenses.

 

Additionally, the application offers a range of quick-action buttons to enhance usability:

  • Filter: Refine your data view based on specific criteria.
  • Export: Download system details for offline reference in excel format.
  • Refresh: Update the view with the latest system information.
  • Personalization: Customize the interface to suit your preferences.

 

 

 

 

 

 

The system list view can be refined using filters based on various criteria, including:

  • Maintenance Validity
  • Licenses
  • Maintenance Certificate Status
  • Received License Status
  • Received Maintenance Certificate Status
  • Automatic Distribution Status

These filtering options allow users to quickly narrow down the list to focus on the most relevant systems and their statuses.

 

 

 

On clicking of the table item navigation opens the detailed page for a selected system, providing comprehensive information such as:

  • Data Received from the Managed System
  • Data Received from SAP Support Backbone
  • Logs

This detailed view ensures users have all the critical insights they need.

entry.title: 連結決算プロセスにイノベーションを！～SAP S/4HANA for Group Reporting 連載企画 第2弾(全5回)～ 内部取引照合照合編
entry.content:
前回 “SAP S/4HANA for Group Reporting(以降 Group Reporting)“の第1弾として、Group Reportingの製品コンセプトと特長についてご紹介させていただきました。今回の第2弾は内部取引照合編です！
連載企画とし、Group Reportingのコンセプトやメリット、機能などをより深く知って頂けるよう全5回に渡って連載させて頂く予定です。是非、ご一読頂ければと思います。

SAP S/4HANA for Group Reporting 連載企画(全5回アジェンダ)

１．製品コンセプトと特長編　
２．内部取引照合編　※当ブログ
３．データ収集編　※リアルタイムデータ収集とマニュアルデータ収集(WEBベース)
４．連結処理編　※外貨換算、内部取引消去、資本連結など
５．管理連結とレポーティング編　※複数通貨連結、計画連結、セグメント連結など

連結決算において内部取引照合が大きな課題になっていませんか？

昨今、多くの日本企業の海外事業の展開や企業買収が進んでおり、国内外問わず、連結子会社として財務報告することが、非常に多くなっているかと思います。

そのような中、本社にて実施される連結決算業務において、グループ間で発生する内部取引の照合を課題に感じている企業のご担当者様も多いのではないでしょうか？連結決算業務は、期末締めを終えた後、単体決算データを連結パッケージやデータ連携ツールなどにて、収集する作業から始まることが一般的かと思います。集めたデータをもとに、連結処理を行い、発生した内部取引の違算調査のため、本社の連結担当者は関連会社の担当者へメールや電話等で連絡を取り、調整作業を行う必要があります。海外の子会社であれば、時差や言語の壁もあり、担当者と調整を行うまでに、3日、4日と時間がかかることもあるでしょう。そのような内部取引照合の調査時間は、決算早期化を目指す企業にとって大きな足枷となりますし、経理担当者や連結担当者の決算期の残業を増やす要因となるかと思います。SAPソリューションでは、そのような企業の課題を解消するべく、期中から内部取引照合を行い、決算期における担当者の負荷を下げる仕組みをご提供しています。

 

SAP Intercompany Matching & Reconciliationが目指す内部取引照合の特長

それではここからは、SAP Intercompany Matching & Reconciliation（以降ICMR）によって実現できる、期中から内部取引照合を行うための仕組みやメリットをご紹介します。

  1. オンデマンド(リアルタイム)でグループ間取引の照合

一つ目の大きな特長は、冒頭からお伝えしている期中からの内部取引照合がオンデマンドで可能という点です。第1弾の振り返りになりますが、Group Reportingでは、単体会計の環境に連結会計が同居する仕組みになっています。従って、単体会計のデータをそのまま活用し、オンデマンドで連結決算処理に取り掛かることができます。財務会計ソリューションにて、期中や月中にオンデマンドで債権・債務や売上・仕入を伝票レベルで照合し、売り手、買い手の担当者同士で確認、違算を調整することが可能です。そうすることで、月末・期末に連結処理に取り掛かる際には、内部取引消去の違算を最小化させ、決算早期化や効率化に寄与できる仕組みをご提供しています。

  1. 内部取引照合にて違算理由を即時分析

2つ目の特長は、内部取引照合をルールベースで行い、違算の原因を即座に把握できる点です。内部取引照合の実行前に、予めマッチングルールを設定します。このマッチングルールには、グループ間取引の整合性をチェックするルールと順序（下図参照）を登録します。さらに、順序ごとに理由コードを設定することで、内部取引照合時において、違算の分析が容易になります。

下図が、自社の債権と取引相手(グループ会社)の債務を照合する際に設定したマッチングルールの例です。理由コードによって、取引が完全に一致しているのか、差額があるのか、など詳細なステータスを確認することができます。また、差額がある場合、調整仕訳の自動転記を行うことも可能となっており、煩雑な伝票処理を効率化することができます。

　

  1. 照合する勘定科目の組み合わせやNon-SAPからのデータを利用した照合も可能

内部取引照合は債権債務のみならず、費用収益や借入貸付など照合対象となる勘定科目の組み合わせは多岐に渡ります。ICMRでは勘定科目や勘定グループによる組み合わせを定義できるようになっていますので、細かな照合ニーズにも対応可能です。また、Non-SAPを利用しているグループ会社からも必要なデータを連携(ファイルアップロード、API連携)し、ICMRに取り込むことも可能になっています。

内部取引照合のプロセス

ここからは、内部取引照合のプロセスについて、ICMRの機能や業務での使い方のご紹介をします。

グループ間取引のご担当者は、現状での内部取引照合の進捗状況を期中から確認することができます。ご担当者は照合ステータス概要画面から、自社と各関連会社の内部取引照合ステータスを確認します。照合ステータスやマッチングステータスを確認することで、現状における全体の進捗率、照合時の許容範囲を超えている残高、未照合の伝票数を確認することができます。それぞれのグループ会社毎の進捗具合を確認できますので、グループ間取引のご担当者間で優先順位を明確にし、作業を進めることが可能です。

 

また、上図の一覧から取引会社を選択することで、以下のような照合画面へ遷移することができます。

 

上図では例として、左上に自社側の債権、右上に相手先の債務が表示されています。設定次第で、売上と仕入を照合することや、左右を逆にした表示をすることも可能です。下段には照合が行われた取引データが表示されています。照合済みデータに関しては、予め設定されているマッチングルールを元に、照合データが完全に一致しているのか、差額許容範囲内での一致であるのかなどステータスコード（理由コード）が割り当てられます。照合済み取引に関して、差額がある場合には、グループ間取引のご担当者から、調整仕訳の自動転記のためのワークフローを送信することができます。差額の調整仕訳は自社と相手会社のどちらでも自動で起票することができ、ワークフローで管理できます。自動調整転記によって、伝票処理工数の削減が図れ、業務の効率化につながります。内部取引照合においては法人同士の照合の他、事業報告単位（セグメント）や利益センタレベルでの照合（マトリックス照合）も可能です。

ICMRを上手に活用し、グループ間取引を照合するためには、そのキーとなる情報(インボイス番号や発注番号など)が必要です。予め、ルールとして会計伝票上のどこに、それらの情報を入力しておくのかを決め、その運用の徹底が必要となります。内部取引照合のプロセスを誰がいつ実施するのか、可能であればプロセスオーナーを決めて取り組むことで、運用の定着を図ることが出来ます。

仕組みづくりーアーキテクチャのご紹介

 

最後に、ICMRのアーキテクチャについてご紹介します。期中の会計伝票は実績データとしてユニバーサルジャーナル（テーブル名：ACDOCA）へ格納されます。ICMRは個社の会計伝票（テーブル名：ACDOCA）のデータを、マッチング明細（テーブル名：ICADOCM）へ連携し照合処理を行います。マッチング明細では、連携元の会計伝票の項目や明細ごとのマッチングステータス、マッチング伝票番号等が格納されます。また、Non-SAPシステムからのファイルアップロードやAPI連携により、マッチング明細へ直接データをアップロードすることも可能です。

 

まとめ：SAPソリューションで実現できること

ここまで、本ブログにお付き合いいただきましてありがとうございました。最後に本ブログでご紹介したSAPソリューションで実現する内部取引照合プロセスのメリットに関してまとめました。

  • オンデマンドでのグループ間取引の照合
  • 内部取引照合における違算理由の即時分析
  • 金額差異発生時における自動調整伝票起票

SAP Intercompany Matching & Reconciliationの活用によって内部取引照合プロセスをリノベーションすることで、連結決算の効率化・早期化、対監査対応力や内部統制の強化を図ることが可能になります。内部取引照合業務に携わっているご担当者様へ、SAPソリューションのご検討におけるご参考になれば幸いです。次号では、データ収集編として、単体決算データの収集をどのように実施できるのか、一歩踏み込んだ詳細なご紹介をさせていただきます。

A clear approach could involve leveraging data synchronization tools and scripts tailored to your specific use case. For more complex projects, consider hiring dedicated .NET developers to ensure smooth integration and efficient solutions tailored to your needs.

entry.title: OData Provisioning: A New Capability of SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
OData Provisioning facilitates exposing business data and logic from SAP Business Suite systems as OData services on the SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP). This enables customers to create and run user-centric applications seamlessly on SAP Cloud.

Till now, SAP BTP offered OData Provisioning through two services:

  1. SAP BTP, OData Provisioning (available exclusively in the NEO environment):
    This service allows business data and logic developed in SAP Business Suite systems to be exposed as OData services on SAP BTP without requiring additional development efforts.
  2. SAP BTP, serverless runtime (available exclusively in the multi-cloud/Cloud Foundry environment):
    This service also includes OData Provisioning, exposing business data and logic from SAP Business Suite systems as OData services on SAP BTP, eliminating the need for extra development.

Since the deprecation of SAP BTP, Serverless Runtime was announced in Q3 2021, and with the SAP BTP NEO environment scheduled to sunset on December 31, 2028 (refer to SAP Note 3351844), we are pleased to announce that the OData Provisioning functionality is now available as a capability of SAP Integration Suite in its Standard, Premium, and Enterprise Agreement editions.  Additionally, it is available with trial and freemium plans.

Activating OData Provisioning capability from SAP Integration Suite

If you are using the Standard, Premium, or Enterprise editions of SAP Integration Suite, you can activate the OData Provisioning capability.

To do so, login to the SAP Integration Suite application as an Integration Provisioner, and in the Capabilities sections, select the Manage Capabilities tile. On the following screen, choose Add Capabilities and then select the option Access data in SAP Business Suite to proceed with activating the OData Provisioning capability.

Then choose Activate to confirm your selection:

Once OData Provisioning is activated, a corresponding tile will appear on the Home page. However, to access the functionality of OData Provisioning, you must assign suitable roles to users.

You will need the following roles to access the OData Provisioning functionality:

  • ODPManage
  • ODPAPIAccess

 Add these roles to a role collection in your sub-account in the BTP cockpit and assign the role collection to the user.

Once you have these roles and have logged in again, you can find the following features:

Configure > OData Services: View and register OData services. 

Monitor > OData Services: View the errors logged for the technical and registered OData services calls and view details like timestamp, service name, namespace, description of the error, and the transaction ID.

Settings > OData Services: Manage metadata validation and cache settings.

The documentation for OData Provisioning in SAP Integration Suite can be found here: OData Provisioning in SAP Integration Suite.

If you are currently using OData Provisioning (NEO) or OData Provisioning as part of SAP BTP, serverless runtime, and already have subscriptions to any of the Standard, Premium, or Enterprise Agreement editions, we recommend planning your transition to OData Provisioning within the SAP Integration Suite.

For assistance, refer to the following transition guides:

  • OData Provisioning (NEO) to OData Provisioning capability in SAP Integration Suite on a Multi-Cloud Foundation Environment
  • Transitioning OData Provisioning from SAP BTP, Serverless Runtime to SAP Integration Suite

If you are new to OData Provisioning, you can explore its functionality through the SAP Integration Suite's trial version or freemium plan.

entry.title: Triggering SAP Build Process Automation Processes from SAP Cloud Application Programming (CAP) Apps
entry.content:
In this blog post, we'll explore the different methods to trigger SAP Build Process Automation (BPA) processes from a SAP Cloud Application Programming (CAP) application. We will cover triggering via destinations and Event Mesh.

Table of Contents

1. Triggering via Destinations

    • Prerequisites
    • Without Principal Propagation
    • With Principal Propagation

2. Triggering via Event Mesh

    • Setting Up Event Mesh
    • Triggering via Event Mesh
    • Using CloudEvents
    • Testing via Postman

 

 

Triggering via Destinations

Prerequisites

Before starting, ensure you have:

  • A CAP application in place with the destination service configured
  • A deployed BPA process
  • A service instance and service key created for SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA)

Without Principal Propagation

Follow these steps to trigger a BPA process without principal propagation:

1. Create a destination to the BPA instance using OAuth2ClientCredentials:

    • In your subaccount, navigate to Destinations and create a new destination.
    • Enter the required details such as Name, Type (HTTP), base URL, and select OAuth2ClientCredentials as the Authentication Method.
    • Provide the necessary Client ID, Client Secret, and Token Service URL.

2. Set up the destination in package.json:

    • Define the destination in your CAP application's package.json under the cds.requires section.

 

"cds": {
	"requires": {
		"bpa_destination": {
			"kind": "rest",
			"credentials": {
				"destination": "<destinationname>"
			}
		}
	}
}

 

3. Connect to the destination via cds.connect.to(destinationname):

    • Use the following code snippet in your CAP service or handler file to connect to the destination:

 

const { cds } = require('@sap/cds');
const bpa_destination = await cds.connect.to("<destinationname>");

 

4. Call the API: Use the connection to make a POST request to the BPA API endpoint:

 

let payload = {
	definitionId: "<myProcessID>",
	context: {
		<StringName1>: "<InputString1>",
		<IntegerName2>: <SampleInteger>
      }
};
let oResult = await bpaService.tx(req).post('/v1/workflow-instances', payload);

if (response.status !== 201) {
	throw new Error('Failed to trigger the process.');
}

 

Note: Adding the URL suffix is optional if the full URL is provided in the destination or in the destination configuration inside the package.json

 

Using Principal Propagation

If you need to propagate user information:

1a. Set up trust between subaccounts (if BPA and CAP are in different subaccounts):

  • See SAP Help Documentation for establishing trust between subaccounts.

1b. Create a destination with OAuth2UserTokenExchange:

Similar to the OAuth2ClientCredentials setup, but select OAuth2UserTokenExchange as the Authentication Method.

  1. Verify API call with principal propagation:
    • Ensure the API call is made with the propagated user context from the request body.
  2. Coding remains the same as without principal propagation:
    • Follow the same code snippet as above.

Note: When making changes to the destination, it can take a few minutes until the changes are active. To shorten this timeframe, you can delete and re-create the destination.

 

 

Triggering via Event Mesh

Before trying to trigger events, ensure that the Event Mesh service is correctly set up, including creating a client, service descriptor and at least one queue.

Triggering via Event Mesh

1. Create custom event specification for SAP Build Process Automation

To create a custom event you need to create an event specification that complies with the CloudEvents specification. This file needs to be stored as a .json file. Here is a generic sample of how this event specification could look like: 

 

{
  "asyncapi": "2.0.0",
  "info": {
    "title": "Generic Event",
    "version": "1.0",
    "description": "This event is triggered by a generic rule engine using custom rules."
  },
  "x-api-type": "EVENT",
  "x-shortText": "Informs a subscriber about a relevant event for a process.",
  "x-stateInfo": { "state": "active" },
  "channels": {
    "generic/event/channel": {
      "subscribe": {
        "message": {
          "description": "myMessageDescription",
          "$ref": "#/components/messages/generic_event_v1"
        }
      }
    }
  },
  "components": {
    "messages": {
      "generic_event_v1": {
        "name": "myMessageName",
        "summary": "mySummary",
        "description": "myDescription",
        "headers": {
          "properties": {
            "type": {
              "const": "<messageType>"
            },
            "datacontenttype": { "const": "application/json" }
          }
        },
        "payload": {
          "$ref": "#/components/schemas/generic_event_v1"
        },
        "traits": [{ "$ref": "#/components/messageTraits/CloudEventContext" }]
      }
    },
    "schemas": {
      "generic_event_v1": {
        "type": "object",
        "properties": {
          "PROPERTY_1": { "type": "string" },
          "PROPERTY_2": { "type": "string" },
          "PROPERTY_3": { "type": "string" }
        }
      }
    },
    "messageTraits": {
      "CloudEventContext": {
        "headers": {
          "type": "object",
          "properties": {
            "specversion": {
              "type": "string",
              "enum": ["1.0"]
            },
            "type": {
              "description": "Type of occurrence which has happened. Often this property is used for routing, observability, policy enforcement, etc.",
              "type": "string",
              "minLength": 1
            },
            "source": { "type": "string" },
            "subject": { "type": "string" },
            "id": { "type": "string" },
            "time": {
              "type": "string",
              "format": "date-time"
            },
            "datacontenttype": {
              "description": "Describe the data encoding format",
              "type": "string",
              "const": "application/json"
            }
          },
          "required": ["id", "specversion", "source", "type"]
        }
      }
    }
  }
}

 

2. Create custom event trigger inside SAP Build Process Automation

To create a custom event in BPA, you have to perform the following steps:

1. Open the SAP Build lobby.

2. Open Connectors > Actions in the menu.

3. Click the Create button and upload your previously created .json file.

4. Release and publish your event

5. Set your new event as a trigger for your process inside the process editor

 

Setup Event Mesh webhook:

To set up the connection between Event Mesh and SAP Build Process Automation, you need to set up a webhook. Webhooks are POST calls that are automatically triggered for each event inside the queue. See this blog post for more information about webhooks.

The following steps are necessary to set up your webhook:

1. Open the client inside your Event Mesh instance.

2. Click on the webhooks tab.

3. Create a new webhook by providing a name, inserting the webhook URL (your BPA endpoint URL in your service key + "/internal/be/v1/events") and the authentication type OAuth2ClientCredentials using the client key and the client secret from the BPA service key.

4. Exempt the handshake and resume the webhook.

 

Trigger events in CAP using messaging.emit():

Use the messaging.emit() function to emit events from CAP to Event Mesh.

 

const messaging = await cds.connect.to('messaging');

await messaging.emit({
        event: '<eventName>',
        data: <inputDataObject>,
        headers: { type: '<messageType>' },
});

 

Note: The data object needs to comply with the CloudEvents standard. See this blog post for more information.

 

Testing Event Trigger via REST-Client

To test the Event Mesh and Event Trigger setup we can test via REST-Clients (such as Postman). This needs to be configured accordingly:

1. Use the following endpoint for Event Mesh

    • Endpoint: <yourEventMeshEndpoint>

You can find the endpoint in your Event Mesh instance service key under messaging. Make sure it contains the pubsub part and that it looks similar to this one: https://enterprise-messaging-pubsub.cfapps.<region>.hana.ondemand.com/messagingrest/v1/messages

2. Set the following headers:

    • Content-Type: application/cloudevents+json
    • x-qos: 1
    • x-address: queue:<queueName>

3. Fetch a Bearer Token for authentication:

    • Ensure you have a valid Bearer Token for authorization. Get one using the grant type Authorization Code.

4. Send a POST request with the CloudEvent payload:

    • Construct and send a POST request to the Event Mesh endpoint.

 

{
  "specversion": "1.0",
  "type": "<messageType>",
  "source": "<queue>/manual",
  "id": "740f8e3e-6b99-40ae-bdc3-375277cc2edb",
  "time": "2024-01-01T12:00:00Z",
  "data": {
    // Your SAP Build Process Automation context here
  },
  "datacontenttype": "application/json"
}

 

 

By following these guidelines, you can trigger SAP Build Process Automation processes from a SAP CAP application using various methods. This approach can greatly enhance the integration and automation capabilities within a SAP landscape.

entry.title: SAP Datasphere - Usage of Meta Chain
entry.content:
In the latest wave (2024.23) SAP introduced nesting of Task Chains and sharing to other spaces. When shared, they can be nested within another Task Chain in that space. In BW this nesting is a known functionality, where a meta chain is a combination of several process chains, which are executed in a predefined order. In Datasphere a Task Chain typically could contain data flows, view persistencies, removing view persistencies, intelligent lookups, replication flows, transformation flows and deleting records with change type “Deleted”. Now task chains join the list. This brings some new advantages.

Data Availability
When an object fails, the following ones in that sequence will not be triggered. This principle was already active in Task Chains. It allows the user to protect persistencies of higher views (in other spaces) in case of failure. A new level is reached!
E.g.: Loading of data using task chain 1, Transformations done in task chain 2. Transformations will not be executed when issues arise within the loading.

Monitoring
Creating these Meta chains in a dedicated space could allow for easier monitoring & re-triggering. Since all task chains are accessible within this space, this is one place to monitor on a high level what tasks are running and if they are successful or not. One could share this one space with 'super'-users that could reactivate these meta chains to update the data, if they need the latest updates.

Orchestration
In the past if Task Chains are dependent of of one another, you would have to see how long they take to run and then leave some margin in the schedule. This is no longer the case.


Let’s give an example for SD:

We start of by creating task chains for the loading of some CDS views (categorized per topic):

When combining these Task Chains you have created you first Meta Chain: Meta Chain Master Data.

In the case of SD we need to load some transactional data like C_SalesDocumentItemDEX_1,... . It is possible that due to business requirements some extra logic/transformations need to be executed. This logic, if too heavy, will need to be persisted. Hence this could be combined in a task chain, especially if logic of one view depends on that of another view. 

This is a second Meta Chain: Meta Chain SD.

Loading the master data first and transactional data after. Here is an example of a complete setup with other modules as well. I've put these in parallel, but if this is too heavy it can stress the system. If this is the case it is recommended to run them sequentially.

!Note: That this is purely an example, and the setup is dependent on many variables (Third party data/SAP data, type of replication, setup of views/queries/analytical model, number of spaces, etc.). It should be taken into consideration that for the moment it is not possible to orchestrate 'continue with the next object on failure'. Which in BW is available. I think this is a matter of time (see remark 2). 

When looking at the Roadmap it seems that more updates will be on their way to Task Chains. Regarding the nested task chains in Q1 2025 it should also be possible to deep retry failed objects in nested Task Chains. Retry latest run will now just trigger failed task chains (instead of re-running failed object within failed task chain). This change will certainly improve efficiency.

How you use these ‘Meta Chains’ is completely up to you! As we have discovered ourselves, there are many ways on how these could be set up to match the environment and requirements.

Feel free to comment and share how you have used this feature to your advantage. Also thanks to my colleagues @SimonVee and @hoerzenbergers for the co-creation of this blog.


Some useful links:

What’s New Datasphere

Requirements for Task Chain Nesting

SAP Datasphere Roadmap

Meta Chain in BW

entry.title: Using BADI IDOC_DATA_INSERT for populating Z fields in VBAK table from IDOC document
entry.content:
Motivation for this post is our experience with appended structures of VBAK and VBAP tables and processing Inbound IDOC documents to populate these new table fields. 

We had fairly simple task: Write dates from inbound IDOC segment E1BPSDHD1 fields CT_VALID_F and CT_VALID_T into the custom Z fields of table VBAK, ZZPOCETAK and ZZKRAJ. This is step by step our suggestion to complete that task.

Step 1.  Append structures with these fields to structures: 

  • BAPE_VBAK – fields

     

     

  • BAPE_VBAKX - flags

     

     

  • VBAKKOZ

  •  

 

  • VBAKKOZX

  •  

     

Step 2. SE18 - Implement BADI IDOC_DATA_INSERT, create a class with suggested name ZCL_IM_IDOC_DATA_INSERT with method from interface IF_EX_IDOC_DATA_INSERT~FILL.

Step 3. Fun part 

Insert two segments E1BPPAREX, with information that needs to be saved in Z fields. Pay special respect to the actual place of your information in IDOC document. In my case I found that segment offset E1BPSDHD1 is not the same as in specification in WE60. Also find exact position of flags and fields for structures BAPE_VBAK and BAPE_VBAKX in E1BPPAREX segment. This segment should fill extensionin table in FM IDOC_INPUT_COD_SALESORDER_CREA. Do not increment segment number, segment number should be max number in your IDOC DATA table.

method if_ex_idoc_data_insert~fill.
*------------------------------------------------------------
* Purpose: Add custom segments to the IDoc data based on existing segments.
* Following code is only for creating new documents. If you want to update existing, you will need VBELN field
*------------------------------------------------------------

data: ls_new_entry       type idoc_insert,
      ls_idoc_data       type edid4,
      lv_max_segnum      type edi_segnum,
      lv_parent_segnum   type edi_segnum,
      lv_hlevel          type edi_hlevel,
      lv_valid_from      type char08,
      lv_valid_to        type char08,
      lv_bape_vbak       type char60,
      lt_new_entries     type tab_idocins,
      lv_have_to_change  type c length 1.

  field-symbols: <fs_idoc_data> type edid4.

*------------------------------------------------------------
* Find the highest segment number in the existing IDoc data
*------------------------------------------------------------
  lv_max_segnum = '000000'.

  loop at data assigning <fs_idoc_data>.
    if <fs_idoc_data>-segnum > lv_max_segnum.
      lv_max_segnum = <fs_idoc_data>-segnum.
    endif.
  endloop.

*------------------------------------------------------------
* Read the parent segment 'E1BPSDHD1' to get its 'SEGNUM' and 'HLEVEL'
*------------------------------------------------------------
  read table data into ls_idoc_data with key segnam = 'E1BPSDHD1'.

  if sy-subrc = 0.

*------------------------------------------------------------
* Extract 'Valid From' and 'Valid To' dates from positions in 'SDATA'
*------------------------------------------------------------
    lv_valid_from = ls_idoc_data-sdata+286(8).  " Start position 286, length 8
    lv_valid_to   = ls_idoc_data-sdata+294(8).  " End   position 294, length 8

*------------------------------------------------------------
* Prepare the first new segment 'E1BPPAREX'
*------------------------------------------------------------
    lv_bape_vbak = |{ 'BAPE_VBAK' width = 60 }|.  " Pad 'BAPE_VBAK' to 60 characters

    concatenate lv_bape_vbak lv_valid_from lv_valid_to into ls_new_entry-sdata respecting blanks.

  " Segment number for the new segment is max number in data table
    ls_new_entry-segnum  = lv_max_segnum.
    ls_new_entry-segnam  = 'E1BPPAREX'.
    ls_new_entry-counter = '000'.

  " Append the new segment to 'lt_new_entries'
    append ls_new_entry to lt_new_entries.

    clear ls_new_entry.

*------------------------------------------------------------
* Prepare the second new segment 'E1BPPAREX'
* Flag structure is 30 in lenght and then you add number of filds with no blanks
* In this case 30 + 10 + 3 and then two XX for flags struc. BAPE_VBAKX
*------------------------------------------------------------
    ls_new_entry-sdata = 'BAPE_VBAKX                                 XX'.

  " Same segment number for the second new segment
    ls_new_entry-segnum  = lv_max_segnum.
    ls_new_entry-segnam  = 'E1BPPAREX'.
    ls_new_entry-counter = '000'.

  " Append the second new segment to 'lt_new_entries'
    append ls_new_entry to lt_new_entries.

*------------------------------------------------------------
* Update protocol data
*------------------------------------------------------------
    protocol-stamid = 'ABVC'.
    protocol-stamno = '123'.
    protocol-repid  = sy-repid.

* Indicate that changes have been made
    have_to_change = 'X'.

*------------------------------------------------------------
* Insert new entries after the parent segment in the IDoc data
*------------------------------------------------------------
    if sy-subrc = 0.
* If parent segment not found, append to the end
      append lines of lt_new_entries to new_entries.
    endif.

  else.
* Parent segment 'E1BPSDHD1' not found, handle accordingly
  " [Optional] Log an error or take alternative action
  endif.

  endmethod.


 I hope this will save some time.

entry.title: Delta processing inside SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Many users are facing an issue on how to reduce records to be processed in their Data & Analytics platform, if the source object is not delta capable. That means, they have to pull the data per full from source system and then process all records per full inside their Data & Analytics platform. In this article I would like to show you, how to reduce records to be processed inside of SAP Datasphere, so that only new, changed and deleted records will be processed and Change Data Capture can be used for the next data processing via Transformation Flow.

These are the steps need to be implemented in SAP Datasphere:

In our case the remote table and the data integration into the first Local Table "rw_cskt" in SAP Datasphere are already done. Here is the property of the Local Table "rw_cskt":

1. Create a SQL View "vw_rw_cskt_delta_test" to extract only new, modified and deleted records from source Local Table "rw_cskt"

Source code:

/*Modified records*/
select
source.row as "source_row",
target.row as "target_row",
source."mandt",source."spras",source."kokrs",source."kostl",source."datbi",source."ktext",source."ltext",source."mctxt",source."operation_flag",source."is_deleted",source."datvon", source."recordstamp", case when source."row" = target."row" then '' else 'U' end as "Change_Type", now() as "Change_Date"
from
(
select
HASH_MD5(to_binary(coalesce("mandt", '')), to_binary(coalesce("spras", '')), to_binary(coalesce("kokrs", '')), to_binary(coalesce("kostl", '')), to_binary(coalesce("datbi", '')), to_binary(coalesce("ktext", '')), to_binary(coalesce("ltext", '')), to_binary(coalesce("mctxt", '')), to_binary(coalesce("operation_flag", '')), to_binary(coalesce("is_deleted", '')), to_binary(coalesce("datvon", ''))) as row,
HASH_MD5(to_binary(coalesce("mandt", '')), to_binary(coalesce("spras", '')), to_binary(coalesce("kokrs", '')), to_binary(coalesce("kostl", '')), to_binary(coalesce("datbi", ''))) as "key",
"mandt","spras","kokrs","kostl","datbi","ktext","ltext","mctxt","operation_flag","is_deleted","recordstamp","datvon"
from "rw_cskt"
) as source
inner join
(
select
HASH_MD5(to_binary(coalesce("mandt", '')), to_binary(coalesce("spras", '')), to_binary(coalesce("kokrs", '')), to_binary(coalesce("kostl", '')), to_binary(coalesce("datbi", '')), to_binary(coalesce("ktext", '')), to_binary(coalesce("ltext", '')), to_binary(coalesce("mctxt", '')), to_binary(coalesce("operation_flag", '')), to_binary(coalesce("is_deleted", '')), to_binary(coalesce("datvon", ''))) as row,
HASH_MD5(to_binary(coalesce("mandt", '')), to_binary(coalesce("spras", '')), to_binary(coalesce("kokrs", '')), to_binary(coalesce("kostl", '')), to_binary(coalesce("datbi", ''))) as "key"
from "rw_cskt_Delta"
) as target on
source.key = target.key
where
source."row" != target."row"

union all

/*New records*/
select
source.row as "source_row",
target.row as "target_row",
"mandt","spras","kokrs","kostl","datbi","ktext","ltext","mctxt","operation_flag","is_deleted","datvon","recordstamp", 'D' as "Change_Type", now() as "Change_Date"
from
(
select
HASH_MD5(to_binary(coalesce("mandt", '')), to_binary(coalesce("spras", '')) ,to_binary(coalesce("kokrs", '')), to_binary(coalesce("kostl", '')), to_binary(coalesce("datbi", ''))) as row,
"mandt","spras","kokrs","kostl","datbi","ktext","ltext","mctxt","operation_flag","is_deleted","recordstamp","datvon"
from
"rw_cskt"
) as source
left outer join
(
select
HASH_MD5(to_binary(coalesce("mandt", '')), to_binary(coalesce("spras", '')) ,to_binary(coalesce("kokrs", '')), to_binary(coalesce("kostl", '')), to_binary(coalesce("datbi", ''))) as row
from
"rw_cskt_Delta"
) as target on
source.row = target.row
where
target.row is null

union all

/*Deleted records*/
select
source.row as "source_row",
target.row as "target_row",
"mandt","spras","kokrs","kostl","datbi","ktext","ltext","mctxt","operation_flag","is_deleted","datvon","recordstamp", 'D' as "Change_Type", now() as "Change_Date"
from
(
select
HASH_MD5(to_binary(coalesce("mandt", '')), to_binary(coalesce("spras", '')) ,to_binary(coalesce("kokrs", '')), to_binary(coalesce("kostl", '')), to_binary(coalesce("datbi", ''))) as row,
"mandt","spras","kokrs","kostl","datbi","ktext","ltext","mctxt","operation_flag","is_deleted","recordstamp","datvon"
from
"rw_cskt_Delta"
) as target
left outer join
(
select
HASH_MD5(to_binary(coalesce("mandt", '')), to_binary(coalesce("spras", '')) ,to_binary(coalesce("kokrs", '')), to_binary(coalesce("kostl", '')), to_binary(coalesce("datbi", ''))) as row
from
"rw_cskt"
) as source on
source.row = target.row
where
source.row is null

In this source code I transform key and all columns in each row (source table and target table) to binary and then to hash MD5 format. And then I check, if there are modifications on existing records in source and target table. In this case Change Type will be set 'U'.

New records will be identify by comparing key columns in the source and target table. If records are not available in target table, then this record will be inserted (Change Type = 'L').

To identify deleted records I transform only key columns in each row (source table and target table) to binary and then to hash MD5 format. If a record in target table is not available in source table, then this record has to be deleted (Change Type = 'D').

The benefit using these functions to_binary and then hash_md5 is, that its conversion and search performance is very fast and the coding is more simple.

If I execute this SQL View, it delivers in this case two records (a modified and a deleted record).

 

2. Use this SQL View "vw_rw_cskt_delta_test" as data source for a new Transformation Flow "tf_rw_cskt_Delta"

3. Create a new Local Table as target with Delta Capture active

Map the "Change Type" and the field "Change Date" can not be mapped. It will be filled automatically by SAP Datasphere.

Since View is not Delta Capture capable, so I can only choose Load Type: Initial only.

4. Execute Transformation Flow

After deploying the Transformation Flow, I execute it and receive this result:

Only two records are being processed by Transformation Flow.

 

Summary:

I hope this approach helps minimize the volume of records to be processed in SAP Datasphere, especially when dealing with hundreds of millions of records. After processing the data, you can use the target local table as a data source for further delta-capable transformation flows, business logic processing, or other purposes

 

Thanks

Wanda Soetomo

entry.title: Exclude data of table based on the selection made in another table
entry.content:
Problem statement:

To display the Inverse of the data from first table in second table.

Solution:

This can be achieved via scripting by using getDataSelections() along with setDimensionFilter() and a property called exclude. This setting will let us display the excluded data made from the selection from table 1 in table 2.

Table 2 showing the inverse of data selected in table 1

Logic:

Step 1:

Create 2 table widgets in the SAC story. I am using the SAP sample dataset for this demo BestRunJuice_SampleModel.

Step 2:

Create necessary Page level filters (Input Controls) for affecting table 1 alone.
I have created 4 Input controls. (Sales manager, Date, Product and Store).

Step 3:

Apply linked Analysis to all 4 input controls by linking table 1 alone.

Step 4:

In the onResultChanged() event of table 1 create the following script.

The idea is to create an array of selection for capturing the data selections from Table 1 and to iterate the elements from the array of selection into an array of string. Then we need to set a dimension filter to selectively filter the table 2 based on the exclusion of members from the initial selection.

 

//getting the selection into an array of selection
var arr_table_selection = Tab1.getDataSource().getDataSelections();
var arr_value=ArrayUtils.create(Type.string);

//push elements from array of selection into an array of string
for(var i=0;i<arr_table_selection.length;i++)
	{
		arr_value.push(arr_table_selection[i]["Sales_Manager__5w3m5d06b5"]);
	}

// Apply Exclusion to Table 2
Tab2.getDataSource().setDimensionFilter("Sales_Manager__5w3m5d06b5", {values: arr_value, exclude: true});

 

This script will be called every time there is a change to the result of table1. For example: any time you make a change to the table 1 with the input filters, the inverse of the available members shown in table1 will be displayed in Table 2.

Since I have selected Sales Manager as the dimension filter. This exclusion logic will be shown on the basis of Sales manager.

Refer the below documentation on how to better use the SetDimensionFilter on different parameters.

Use setDimensionFilter API on Range and Exclude Filters | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Email validation - functioning email account behind the address
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

we would like to automatically validate the email addresses in the master data (customer master) within an SAP system (using an embedded SAP MDG on top). This goes beyond a simple syntax check of the email addresses (e.g., ensuring an "@" is included, that it ends with a valid top-level domain like "sap.com," contains no special characters, etc.). We are also interested in verifying whether a functioning email account exists behind the address. Technically, this cannot of course always be determined with 100% certainty, as not all mail servers consistently return error messages when an email cannot be delivered to an account. However, we would view this as an additional heuristic data quality check, which, for example, could also be provided by third-party vendors (e.g. https://www.abstractapi.com/api/email-verification-validation-api). We are not aware of any standard functionality in the S/4HANA core and/or in SAP MDG, which is why our current approaches are based on extensibility and/or integration tools:

  • SAP Integration Suite: call to such an API in an existing flow
  • SAP Build Process Automation: via task automation and external action
  • ABAP (on-stack)/RAP: external HTTPS API call
  • Side-by-side app/middleware using an arbitrary programming language and/or framework (e.g. CAP)

Do you have any best practices, ideas, and/or experience to share in this topic? We would highly appreciate your feedback!

 

Best regards,
Nicolas

 

SAP S/4HANA SAP Master Data Governance SAP Business One Extensibility

entry.title: SAP Cloud ALM is now available in EU Access
entry.content:
I am pleased to announce that SAP Cloud ALM can now be provisioned in EU Access.

SAP has opened a new EU Access certified data center for SAP Cloud ALM, it is located in Germany.

Only customers already owning a valid EU Access contract with other SAP products will be allowed to provision SAP Cloud ALM in EU Access.

If, as a customer, you own a valid EU Access contract, then your SAP Cloud ALM entitlement set will be marked as EU Access. When it is a the case a new data center will appear in the provisioning screen in SAP for me, as shown in the picture below:

Region selection with EU Access data center for an EU Access entitlement set

Please note that there is no obligation to provision SAP Cloud ALM in EU Access, it is up to you to select the appropriate data center if you wish to do so.

If you select the EU Access data center, as indicated by the text in the drop down box, you must select and SAP Cloud Identity Services tenant which is also located in the EU; otherwise your SAP Cloud ALM tenant will not be EU Access compliant.

SAP is currently working on a solution which will prevent the user from selecting an SAP Cloud Identity Services tenant which is not in the EU, this will be available in 2025.

Full details can be found on the SAP Cloud ALM provisioning help page.

entry.title: Re: sending internal table data in xlsx file via mail
entry.content:
Hi,

Please look the below code for sending the internal table records to excel file to SOST mail.

* Declarations

CONSTANTS: gc_tab  TYPE c VALUE cl_bcs_convert=>gc_tab,
           gc_crlf TYPE c VALUE cl_bcs_convert=>gc_crlf.

    DATA: send_request       TYPE REF TO cl_bcs,
          ls_text            TYPE soli,                    " Text  internal table
          lt_text            TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF soli,  " Text  internal table
          wa_text            TYPE soli,                    " Text wrokarea table
          document           TYPE REF TO cl_document_bcs,
          sender             TYPE REF TO cl_sapuser_bcs,
          recipient          TYPE REF TO if_recipient_bcs,
          bcs_exception      TYPE REF TO cx_bcs,
          sent_to_all        TYPE os_boolean,              "Boolean
          size               TYPE so_obj_len,
          binary_content     TYPE solix_tab,
          lv_string          TYPE char255,
          lv_excel_data      TYPE string,
          ls_document_bcs    TYPE REF TO cx_document_bcs VALUE IS INITIAL,
          lt_return          TYPE TABLE OF bapiret2,
          lv_subject         TYPE so_obj_des.

*   get the po details
    SELECT bnfpo,
           loekz,
           frgzu,
           txz01,
           matnr,
           ematn,
           menge,
           ebeln,
           ebelp,
           mfrpn
           FROM eban INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_eban)
           WHERE  ebeln = '1234567990'.

    IF lt_eban IS INITIAL.

      SELECT ebeln,
             ebelp,
             loekz,
             txz01,
             matnr,
             ematn,
             menge,
             mfrpn
      FROM ekpo INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_ekpo)
      WHERE ebeln = '9999999999'.

      IF lt_ekpo IS NOT INITIAL.
        SELECT ebeln,
               loekz,
               lponr,
               frgzu
           FROM ekko INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_ekko)
          FOR ALL ENTRIES IN @LT_ekpo
           WHERE ebeln = @LT_ekpo-ebeln.
      ENDIF.



      IF lt_ekpo IS NOT INITIAL.

*       Headings for excel file
        CONCATENATE lv_excel_data
                    'PO Number' gc_tab
                    'PO Item' gc_tab
                    'Material Code' gc_tab
                    'Description' gc_tab
                    'MPN' gc_crlf
                    INTO lv_excel_data.
.
*       fill excell file multiple items
        LOOP AT lt_ekpo ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_ekpo>).

          READ TABLE lt_ekko ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_ekko>)
                             WITH KEY ebeln = <fs_ekpo>-ebeln.
          IF sy-subrc = 0.

            CONCATENATE lv_excel_data
                 <fs_ekko>-ebeln  gc_tab
                 <fs_ekpo>-ebelp  gc_tab
                 <fs_ekpo>-matnr  gc_tab
                 <fs_ekpo>-txz01  gc_tab
                 <fs_ekpo>-mfrpn  gc_crlf
                 INTO lv_excel_data.
          ENDIF.
        ENDLOOP.

*       Convert string to binary
        TRY.
            cl_bcs_convert=>string_to_solix(
              EXPORTING
                iv_string   = lv_excel_data
                iv_codepage = '4103'  "suitable for MS Excel, leave empty
                iv_add_bom  = 'X'     "for other doc types
              IMPORTING
                et_solix    = binary_content
                ev_size     = size ).
          CATCH cx_bcs.
            MESSAGE e445(so).
        ENDTRY.


          TRY.
*             Create Persistant Class
              send_request = cl_bcs=>create_persistent( ).

*             Create Document
              lv_subject = |Mail Subject|.

              ls_text-line = |<html><body>  </body></html>|.
              APPEND ls_text TO lt_text.

              ls_text-line = |<p> Dear Sir/Madam </p>|.
              APPEND ls_text TO lt_text.

              ls_text-line = space.
              APPEND ls_text TO lt_text.

              lv_string = |<p>maintaine content whatever you want </p>|.
              ls_text-line = lv_string.
              APPEND ls_text TO lt_text.
              CLEAR lv_string.

              lv_string = |<P>This is system generated email don't reply,</P>|.
              ls_text-line = lv_string.
              APPEND ls_text TO lt_text.
              CLEAR : ls_text, lv_string.

              lv_string = |<P>Thank You. </P>|.
              ls_text-line = lv_string.
              APPEND ls_text TO lt_text.
              CLEAR : ls_text, lv_string.


*             Creates an Office Document
              document = cl_document_bcs=>create_document(
                i_type        = 'HTM'
                i_text        = lt_text[]
                i_sensitivity = '1'
                i_importance  = 'O'
                i_subject     = lv_subject ).

*             Add the po data in  excel attachement
              document->add_attachment(
                i_attachment_type    = 'xls'
                i_attachment_subject = 'Excel_name'
                i_attachment_size    = size
                i_att_content_hex    = binary_content ).

              CALL METHOD send_request->set_document( document ).

*             Set Sender
              sender = cl_sapuser_bcs=>create( sy-uname ).
              CALL METHOD send_request->set_sender
                EXPORTING
                  i_sender = sender.

*             Add Recipient Email ID
              recipient = cl_cam_address_bcs=>create_internet_address(
                'maintaine mail ID' ).

*             Send Request
              CALL METHOD send_request->add_recipient
                EXPORTING
                  i_recipient = recipient
                  i_express   = 'X'.

*             Send Email
              CALL METHOD send_request->send(
                EXPORTING
                  i_with_error_screen = 'X'
                RECEIVING
                  result              = sent_to_all ).

              COMMIT WORK.
*           Exception Handling
            CATCH cx_bcs INTO bcs_exception.
              WRITE: TEXT-001.
              EXIT.
          ENDTRY.
        ENDIF.
      ENDIF.

Regards,

Kasireddy M.

entry.title: SAP Data Migration Greenfield vs Brownfield vs Bluefield, which one to pick?
entry.content:
Hello All,

Today's topic is on the which type of project you deal?

 

SAP S/4HANA transformation involves migrating from an older SAP system (like ECC) or a non-SAP system to the modern S/4HANA platform. There are three main approaches to this transformation: Greenfield, Brownfield, and Bluefield. Here’s a simple explanation with examples:

 

1. Greenfield Approach (Start Fresh)

This approach is like building a new house. You start from scratch, designing a completely new system and processes on S/4HANA.

Characteristics:

  • Implement S/4HANA as a completely new system.
  • No historical baggage; adopt best practices.
  • Ideal for businesses looking to reimagine their processes or who have overly complex legacy systems.

Example:

A company running its operations on SAP ECC decides to completely redesign its supply chain and finance processes to align with SAP S/4HANA best practices. They do not migrate historical data but selectively upload only essential master data.

 

2. Brownfield Approach (System Conversion)

This is like renovating your existing house. You upgrade your existing SAP system to S/4HANA, keeping most of the existing configurations and data.

Characteristics:

  • Direct conversion from SAP ECC to S/4HANA.
  • Retain existing business processes, customizations, and historical data.
  • Suitable for businesses with stable processes and extensive legacy investments.

Example:

A manufacturing company running SAP ECC upgrades to S/4HANA while retaining its existing production planning and material management processes. They convert their current database and configurations to S/4HANA with minimal disruption.

 

3. Bluefield Approach (Selective Data Transition)

This approach is like renovating part of your house and building an extension. You keep some legacy systems, processes, or data and adopt new ones where needed.

Characteristics:

  • Migrate only selected data and processes to S/4HANA.
  • A hybrid between Greenfield and Brownfield approaches.
  • Allows for process optimization while retaining critical historical data.

Example:

A global company with operations in the US and Europe decides to migrate its finance systems to S/4HANA using best practices (Greenfield style) while retaining historical transaction data for compliance (Brownfield style). They selectively move their core processes while improving others.

 

Comparison at a Glance

AspectGreenfieldBrownfieldBluefield
ApproachFresh implementationSystem upgrade/conversionSelective data/process migration
Data MigrationOnly essential dataFull historical dataSelective historical data
CustomizationNew configurationsRetain existing customizationsCombination of new and old
Use CaseProcess reimaginationStable processesBalancing optimization and legacy
 
Choosing the right approach depends on your organization’s goals, existing system complexity, and budget. Let me know if you need further details!
 
Thanks,
Venkatesh Golla

entry.title: Payment run; clearing journal entry
entry.content:
Hi all,

We are working with the latest SAP Public Cloud 2408 release and facing the following problem:

We run the app "Manage automatic payments" for our weekly supplier payments. There are more than 1000 invoices in this payment run which have been cleared. Before sending the file to the bank, we noticed a mistake.

At that point, it is not possible to reverse the payment run because the payment media has been created - even if I delete the payment media. The only solution in SAP Public Cloud is the manual reverse (see Note 2682947).

For each supplier there's a single clearing journal entry. As per now, I have to reverse approx. 200 clearing journal entries manually.

e.g. Supplier A = clearing journal entry 20000000100, Supplier B = clearing journal entry 2000000101, Supplier C = clearing journal entry 2000000102

 

My question: Is there a configuration in the system to generate a clearing journal entry for each payment run?

e.g. Supplier A = clearing journal entry 20000000100, Supplier B = clearing journal entry 2000000100, Supplier C = clearing journal entry 2000000100

Many thanks in advance.

entry.title: Webi 4.3 PDF Output: Empty Page Issue
entry.content:
Symptom

"After migrating the WEBi report from version 4.2 to 4.3, the PDF output generates blank pages, resulting in incomplete or incorrect report delivery.

Environment

  • SAP BusinessObjects Business Intelligence platform 4.3
  • Web Intelligence (Webi)

Reproducing the Issue

  • Migrate the Webi report from version 4.2 to version 4.3. The report contains blocks and sections, has a letter format, and the data should span more than 10 pages.
  • Run the report in Webi 4.3.
  • Export the report output in PDF format.
  • The exported PDF output contains blank pages.

Cause

'' The pages are blank because the data in a particular section has shifted upwards by one row in the document.

To resolve this issue, follow the below steps.

  1. Open the report in BI LaunchPad.
  2. Switch to Design Mode.
  3. Go to Page Setup.
  4. Click on Page, then set the page size to A4.
  5. Save the report and test the behavior again.

Conclusion: This blog helps resolve the issue of the " Empty Page Issue of Webi 4.3 PDF Output.

Feedback and comments are appreciated Thanks for stopping by, and I hope this helps at least a few developers/users out there.,
