entry.title: GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, premium - A Practical Guide
entry.content:
I've previously outlined the entitlements that are part of both base and premium edition in my base edition blog. In this blog, I will focus on the 8 entitlements that are exclusive to the premium edition bundle. I go over what each entitlement does, how much you have, and outline one primary business use case for the functionality. Based on feedback from the last blog, I've included more visuals, and a 'show me' section for each premium entitlement. 

Note: This blog is referring to the global SKU for premium edition, 8014761 that is applicable outside of Greater China region. You can validate if you have the premium edition SKU by logging into SAP for Me: Reporting > Purchased Solutions. Under 'Orders by Solution Area,' select S/4HANA Public - your SKU will be listed on the right. If 8014761 appears with a valid date, you have the GROW Premium Edition. If 8014771 appears, you have Base. Other SKUs likely represent SKUs that do not include this full suite of entitlements.

Outline:

  1. Base Edition vs. Premium Edition
  2. Customer Relationship Management - SAP Sales Cloud Version 2
  3. Spend Management & Business Network - SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management
  4. Spend Management & Business Network - Concur Expense Professional
  5. BTP - SAP Build Apps
  6. BTP - SAC Planning
  7. Finance - Advanced Financial Close
  8. Finance - Receivables Management
  9. Finance - Cash Management
  10. Conclusion

1. Base Edition vs. Premium Edition

The premium edition SKU includes the identical CPEA Credits, BTP Build Process Automation, and Build Workzone entitlements as in the base edition.
The premium edition also includes a Group Reporting entitlement with access to 50 objects vs. 25 in base, and 500K Digital Access documents per annum vs. 100K in base.
See the image below for a side by side overview of base vs. premium entitlements on the global SKUs.


Below, we explore in brief each of the entitlements that are exclusively in the premium edition bundle and were not covered in the base blog.

2. Customer Relationship Management: SAP Sales Cloud Version 2

Business Case
SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 is a CRM system that allows businesses to manage their sales opportunities and customer (and prospective customer) information. Sales teams can see and prioritize their open opportunities, engage with their customer contacts from within the system, and convert these opportunities into orders or projects in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud system. If your business has a sales team that looks for new business opportunities - you have a use case for Sales Cloud V2

Show me
This 3 minute video shows SAP Sales Cloud V2 + SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition in action
Click this App Store or Google Play link to experience a demo on your mobile device.



Business Role Relevance
This will be used by your sales team. Sales Managers, directors, agents etc.
 
Entitlement
15 User Licenses to Sales Cloud V2 was added to the premium edition of GROW in July 2024. This means that for any customer who signed their contract on or after July 15th have this as a part of their original contract.
For customers who purchased GROW premium prior to July 2024, if they did not already have a separate contract for Sales Cloud then SAP offers the chance to retroactively claim this entitlement. To do so, if you have a direct contract with SAP, reach out to your account executive. If your contract is with an SAP Partner, then reach out to your partner manager, who can in turn reach out to their SAP Partner Business Manager if needed. Notes: 1) Claiming the entitlement requires signing a (digital) contract amendment, at no additional cost. 2) You can claim the entitlement now, and implement/adopt later.

License Consumption
As of writing this blog, there is no available metric with which you can track your consumption of Sales Cloud V2 licenses in SAP4ME. However, your license consumption can be viewed within the Service Control Center - which you get access to in Sales Cloud. See a full writeup on how to view this here.

Overusage
It is possible to over-consume your sales cloud user licenses. To purchase more user licenses, engage your SAP contact and enquire about SKU#  8014321.

Scope Item Relevance
The main scope item for Sales Cloud V2 is 6KO - SAP Customer Experience Entities with SAP Service and Sales Cloud. This scope item measures whether or not a communication arrangement has been setup between the ERP and Sales Cloud. 

Key Adoption Notes:
Expertise needed: You'll need a Sales Cloud (CX) skilled consultant for this setup - it's not usually done by an ERP LoB consultant. Additionally you'll need a technical consultant for the integration setup.
Adoption Effort:  Rated 3-4 (on a scale from 1 to 5). This is a full solution, and the complexity can vary significantly depending on the number of combinations etc. you configure for the business. You can get a sense of the implementation scope and effort if SAP services is contracted for the adoption from this page - but of course partner service offerings may vary significantly.

More Information
For more detailed guidance on Sales Cloud V2, this HELP Portal Feature page gives an index of the topics and structures that Sales Cloud covers, while this learning course can allow you a greater amount of depth including seeing configuration options.

3. Spend Management & Business Network - SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management

Business Case
Despite its name, the key value offering from Central Invoice Management (CIM) for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public edition customers isn't the 'centralized' bit - it's the invoice scanning and optical character recognition (OCR) functionality. So rather than employees looking at a paper/pdf-based invoice from your supplier and keying in the relevant information, it's automatically scanned in - and categorized for automatic settling or post-processing as per the rules of your configuration.
Note: CIM also does the central invoice management piece, i.e. if you're running multiple systems you can see all your invoices in one central space - but for most customers the OCR bit is what's immediately relevant.

Show Me
This 3 minute video shows you the CIM OCR functionality in action - processing inbound supplier invoices. In this video, the user is switched between ERP and BTP (CIM is an application running on BTP) so seamlessly that you can't tell when it transitions back and forth - which I think is great for the user experience.

 

Business Role Relevance
This solution is typically used by accounts payable clerks - or whoever in the organization enters and reviews supplier invoices.

Is this solution relevant to your business?
This is applicable to MOST businesses. But you can validate this by:
1. Asking the team in the business responsible for invoice entry how many invoices are manually entered, how much time it takes, and how many repetitive types of invoices they need to enter e.g. from the same supplier with similar items. The more repetitive entries they key in - the greater the value of CIM's OCR functionality.
2. Look at the your used application list in Portfolio & Products / S/4HANA Public - Usage. Scroll down to Most Used Apps. If Supplier Invoice List (Version 2), and Manage Supplier Invoices as heavily used apps in this list, that gives you an idea of how often employees are going in to enter and review invoices.

Entitlement
The premium edition entitles you to 20,000 documents to be processed. I.e. you can have up to 20,000 invoices scanned and converted before needing additional license spend.

License Consumption
You can see your consumption of the allocated 20,000 documents in Reporting - License Consumption, shown under metric CP228 - Integration Suite messages.

Overusage
It is possible to overuse your document allocation. If you need more than the 20,000 license quantity, enquire with your SAP contact about SKUs 8010952 and 8011730 (these are bundled in together in your GROW Premium bundle)

Scope Item Relevance
Scope item 4N6 - Invoice Processing with SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management - measures whether or not you've setup the communication arrangement to exchange data between BTP (where CIM runs) and the ERP. However, in this case - I think looking at the # of documents actually consumed via your CP228 metric is the best indication of CIM usage.

Key Adoption Notes
Expertise Needed: You'll need a BTP/Integration Consultant for the integration setup. You'll also need ERP Finance Consulting (with Accounts Payable Experience) for the functional configuration.
Adoption Effort: 2.5 / 5.
The longer part of the implementation of this until recently (November 2024) has been the BTP setup piece, but as of October 2024 the setup here has been made dramatically faster - the BTP setup piece has gone down from often taking days to being measured in hours. See information about the 'boosters' that have enabled that in this document.
There aren't many functional configurations to be applied - but data entry may be significant depending on the number of company code/vendor combinations the customer has.

Suitability/Localization: There are three dimensions to consider to determine whether CIM works for your business case:
1. UI Language - When you are in the CIM application, in BTP - the UI language determines whether you can see apps, prompts, messages etc. within the system in your desired language. E.g. You can view the CIM application in Japanese, but not in Mandarin Chinese. Here is the full list of available UI Languages.
2. OCR Supported Languages of the Supplier Document: If I log in to the application in Japanese, but the invoice is in Polish - the OCR language speaks to Polish in this case. For this example, you can still expect this to work as Poland is included in the list of document languages - listed here.
3. Country Legal Compliance Localization: To explain via example: A crucial bit of CIM functionality for example is that invoices get 'parked' if all OCR and mapping steps aren't successfully executed. A user then needs to review and release the 'parked' invoice. Some countries, e.g. Italy and Argentina - do not permit parked invoices because of their government's requirements for real-time documentation exchange with the government electronic verification system. There is no standing list of legal compliance matches like this for CIM that I'm aware of, so your compliance or finance experts will need to review for some specific countries.

More Information
This product page provides categories, demo links etc.
This FAQ answers detailed questions such as file size limits, supported languages etc.


4. Spend Management & Business Network - Concur Expense Professional

Business Case
Concur Expense Professional's primary use case is to make it easier for employees to submit expense claims to the business, and for the business to process those expense claims - reimburse the employee, and correctly categorize their expenses. So if an employee is on a business trip to Germany - they can record their expenses such as hotel cost, breakfast/lunch/dinner/entertainment - and enter it along with receipts into the concur app. The app will verify that the expenses fit within policy (e.g. breakfast must be <50$, dinner <100$ etc.), flag it for revision where there are exceptions - and then allow the employee submit the expenses altogether to their employer for post-processing and settlement.

Show Me
This self-guided demo shows you the employee experience of using the app and submitting their expense report (2-4 minutes to run through it).
Alternatively - this video (2 minutes) shows the experience. (Note that in the video, OCR is being used to automatically convert the receipt into entries is a functionality called 'ExpenseIt' that requires additional licensing.)
 

Business Role Relevance
Any employee that incurs expenses on behalf of the company for which they claim reimbursement. This is particularly common for roles with significant travel such as sales, professional services, and senior management roles.

Entitlement
In July 2024, Concur Expense Professional was added to the GROW with SAP premium edition bundle - adding 100 Expense reports per month. Note that a single expense report can contain multiple expenses - so a single business trip with say 15 meals, hotel and airfare all being expensed - can be submitted on a single report and consume 1/100 of the monthly included transactions.
For customers contracted before July 15th 2024: For GROW Premium customers who established their contract before Concur was added to the bundle - SAP is offering customers who do not already have any concur contract the opportunity to retroactively claim this entitlement until December 31st 2024. To claim the entitlement, a contract amendment must be signed (at no cost). Claiming the entitlement doesn't mean you have to implement - you can claim this year and look to implement in 2025/2026 for exmple - but the offer to gain this entitlement without additional cost expires at the end of 2024. Reach out to your SAP Account Executive, or to your partner business manager if you fall into this category and want to claim your entitlement.

License Consumption
SAP4ME doesn’t display your concur entitlement consumption as of writing this blog (2024-11)
However, within Concur you can execute a report that shows the # of reports consumed and details as required. This instruction is shown on pages 45-48 of this document.

Scope Item Relevance
The scope item 1MO - Travel Expense Processing with SAP Concur Expense Professional measures if a communication arrangement has been created to exchange data between SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and Concur. 

Key Adoption Notes
Expertise Needed: Concur must be implemented by a certified implementation partner, or by SAP. SAP offers a 10k$ fixed scope implementation service, and of course partner offerings may vary. The FAQ linked in 'more information' section below provides additional information about this service.

More Information
This FAQ document contains information about the included entitlement,  the implementation setup service,  where to get concur support etc.

5. BTP - SAP Build Apps

Business Case
SAP Build Apps allows you to build mobile and web apps using a drag and drop interface. These apps can include business logic and allow you to do more validation steps than is possible within the ERP, and to bring components from multiple systems together into a new application via integration into Build Apps. This blog provides a spread of different use cases that has been achieved with SAP Build,(which contains Build Apps, Build Process Automation, and Build Workzone) e.g. a mobile app for machine maintenance.

Show Me
This 10 minute video takes you through a full demo of building an app with SAP build apps.
 

 

Business Role Relevance
Build apps can be leveraged by any company employee/worker for whom the app is built.

Entitlement
GROW premium customers are entitled to 25 users - this refers to the number of unique individuals using the application you've built. There are also capacity unit limitations to consider as outlined in the Discovery Center - Pricing area - here.

License Consumption
In SAP4ME section Reporting - License Consumption - the metric CP283 - Active Users - measures the number of unique users consuming your apps. Note: This metric should appear if you've ever consumed Build Apps users (CP283 may not appear in your dashboard if you've never utilized Build Apps).

Overusage
BTP restricts you from overconsuming your allocated users. If you need more than the core entitlement, you can consume CPEA credits for this or purchase more as per the guidance here.

Scope Item Relevance
There is no scope item specifically related to BTP Build apps. You may leverage a variety of API's depending on the ERP functionality you're integrating with.

Key Adoption Notes
Appgyver: If you're familiar with Appgyver, SAP Build Apps has replaced Appgyver and continued to evolve its capabilities.
Adoption Effort: I'd score this at 2-3, as the point of the tool is that it's drag-and-drop friendly. Technical knowledge is still needed to connect to multiple data sources etc., and writing business logic is involved (Gen AI makes this dramatically easier now) - but it is designed to be 'civilian friendly', and has excellent supporting resources available. This mission in particular supports you on the fundamentals.

More Information
Look at the missions for SAP Build Apps on Discovery Center - here. This includes some of the most frequently leveraged use cases that you can follow along to get started.


5. BTP - SAC Planning

Business Case
SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) is a platform that supports a number of activities - the most commonly leveraged one being business intelligence type data modelling and dashboard creation. Whereas SAC is primarily used for historical and current data views - SAC Planning is a featureset that allows you to look at the future piece of the numberline. SAC planning enables you to create and work with future forecasts and planning versions, enabling users to simulate what will happen depending on different assumptions, models, goals etc.

Show Me
This video (5m) provides a walkthrough of why, how and what SAC Planning can do.
 

Business Role Relevance
This depends on the kind of planning that you're doing, but most use cases revolve around financial planning - e.g. for currency, costs, cash flow etc. and so be most valuable for finance managers.

Entitlement
The premium edition includes an entitlement for:
1 professional grade user
10 standard users
A professional user can access all functionalities, including the creation and update of planning models.
Standard users can access and utilize the models, make simulations etc. - they just can't create and edit the planning models themselves. See the list of activities that can be done by standard vs. professional users here. 

License Consumption
In SAP4ME - Reporting - License Consumption:
Measure CH12 tracks how many of the (1 included) Professional Users you're consuming
Measure CH13 tracks how many (10 included)  standard user licenses you're consuming.

Overusage
You are blocked from using more than you've purchased.
You can license additional users as needed as per pricing information here - but note that CPEA credits are not applicable to SAC Planning.

Scope Item Relevance
There is no specific scope item that measures SAC Planning,

Key Adoption Notes
Adoption Effort: Assuming you've got BTP expertise, I'd grade this as 2. There was recently a small hands-on workshop where in the course of 3 hours, customers got up and running successfully building a SAC Planning model.
Skills Needed: BTP/Technical expert (for administration, data model build etc.), and then for most use cases, the relevant functional consultant (often a finance consultant) to structure the data report etc.

More Information
The HELP Documentation menu for baseline SAC - here - is excellent. It includes a licensing breakdown, Planning Specific Area, and Video Library

6. Finance - Receivables Management

Business Case
Basic 'receivables management' is a part of the core ERP, and is covered for example by scope item BD6 - Basic Credit Management. The entitlement for Receivables Management refers to more advanced functionality that is embedded in scope that otherwise requires additional licensing fees: 1QM - Advanced Credit Management and BFC - Collection and Dispute Management. This (advanced) functionality allows the business to categorize customers according to scoring rules, define those scoring rules, and automatically calculate and assign customers specific credit limits on that basis.

Show Me
This 3 minute video shows you the full suite of Receivables Management functionality - including basic + this advanced entitled scope.
 

Business Role Relevance
This functionality is leveraged by your credit controllers and finance accounts payable roles.

Entitlement
GROW premium entitles you to assign the roles that can access this (1QM and BFC) functionality to 5 business users. This is tracked by metric C416 - and this document describes how this metric is calculated.

License Consumption
You can view your consumption of Receivables Management, i.e. the number of users assigned the relevant business role, in SAP4ME - Reporting - License Consumption, Metric C416 shows how many users have the relevant roles.

Overusage
It is possible to assign more users access than you are entitled to. SAP periodically reviews your consumption of this license and will reach out in the case of overconsumption.

Scope Item Relevance
Scope items 1QM - Advanced Credit Management and BFC - Collection and Dispute Management, measures your usage of this advanced receivables management functionality based on whether the business has set up advanced credit calculation rules for 1QM, and whether the system users have accessed the apps needed to leverage the BFC functions such as Manage Dispute Cases.

Key Adoption Notes
1RY - Credit Agency Integration: While the scope item 1RY - Credit Agency isn't a paid scope item, it requires 1QM - Advanced Credit Management in order to be used. As the receivables management functionality of the premium edition subscription entitles you to 1QM, you can also explore 1RY adoption without additional licensing costs.

More Information
This page on sap.com has a number of feature descriptions, value cases, links to services etc.

7. Finance - Cash Management

Business Case
Like for receivables management, basic cash management functionality is included in the core ERP - and is largely consumed via scope items scope items BFA - Basic Bank Account Management and BFB - Basic Cash Operations. The entitlement for cash management covers more advanced bank account and cash management functionalities such as managing bank accounts via a hierarchy, defining approval rules for payment and bank account changes, etc.

Show Me
I've not been able to find a recent video demonstrating the advanced Cash Management functionality with SAP S/4HANA Cloud public edition. I'll update this section when I find one.
 

Business Role Relevance
The business's finance team - the colleagues who manage bank accounts, forecast cash and liquidity etc.

Entitlement
The premium edition entitles you to grant 5 users access to the advanced cash management applications.

License Consumption
You can view how many users have been granted access to the catalogs listed here in SAP4ME - License - Consumption - and look for metric C415.

Overusage
It is possible to assign more than your entitled (or purchased) users the metered catalogs. SAP reviews this periodically and will reach out in cases of overconsumption. To purchase more licenses, enquire with your SAP contact about SKU 8011042

Scope Item Relevance
This entitlement gives access to the apps and functions needed in the following scope items that otherwise require additional licensing:
J77 - Advanced Bank Account Management
J78 - Advanced Cash Operations
And also: 2YM - Application Interface to Retrieve Payment Files, 2O0 - Bank Fee Management, 2U8 - Intercompany Process for Cash Concentration, 3L5 - Liquidity Planning, 34P - Treasury Workstation Cash Integration

Key Adoption Notes
Synergy with SAC Planning: There's a lot of synergy re financial planning forecasts that comes online with Cash Management + SAC Planning, which is also in the premium bundle.

More Information
This deck provides a great (L2) view of the full suite of cash management functionality (Basic + entitled advanced). This site provides highlights of the main categories covered by cash management, links onto available SAP services etc.

8. Finance - Advanced Financial Close

Business Case
Advanced Financial Close is used to centralize and automate the financial close process. It allows defining of advanced rules via workflows, additional compliance checks, and advanced visualization of steps in the closing process. It's most valuable for companies with multiple entities that should follow a centrally defined set of rules and tasks in the closing process.

Show Me
3 Minute Marketing Video - shows you the some of the screens utilized in AFC, and various apps used in the process like Financial Close Overview, Closing Task Completion, Manage Closing Tasks etc.
 

Business Role Relevance
AFC functionality will most likely be used by financial controllers, accountants, etc.

Entitlement
The premium edition entitles you to 50 'objects', where an object is effectively equal to an active company code. See the official definition here.

License Consumption
You can view how many objects have been consumed vs your entitlement in SAP4ME - License - Consumption - and look for metric CY91

Overusage
It is possible to overconsume your allocated objects. SAP reviews your consumption periodically. To purchase additional quantity, enquire with your SAP contact about SKU 8013741

Scope Item Relevance
The scope item 4HG - Advanced Financial Closing Integration, measures whether or not you've set up the communication arrangement necessary to exchange data with AFC.

More Information
This SAP Product Page provides subcategories covered within AFC, and has onward links to installation, configuration etc. pages.
This community page links on to blogs, Q&A area etc.

3. Conclusion

The main objective of these blogs is for the reader to come away with a sense of 'I have a rough understanding of what the components of the bundles are.' That attempted, the two blogs are very long - so next I'll likely to work on a one-pager that brings this all together even more concisely. Stay tuned!

entry.title: BI 4.3 Merge object is not filtering the result set based on source dimension as 4.2
entry.content:
Symptom

"After migrating the WEBi report from version 4.2 to 4.3, the Merge object is not filtering the result set based on source dimension.

Environment

  • SAP BusinessObjects Business Intelligence platform 4.3
  • Web Intelligence (Webi)

Reproducing the Issue

  • Migrate the Webi report from version 4.2 to version 4.3. The report includes a query-on-query filter that uses Max logic to retrieve the record with the latest date from the second query and then applies the filter from the first query based on the result of the second query
  • Run the report in Webi 4.3.
  • The report will get duplicate records instead of distinct records.

Cause

This issue is due to the difference in decimal precision between BO 4.2 and BO 4.3. In BO 4.2, a DateTime object fetches a timestamp with a decimal point (e.g., 1673391774175.8320), while in BO 4.3, the same data is fetched without the decimal portion (e.g., 1673391774175.0000).

To work around this issue, follow the below steps.

  1. Open the report in BI LaunchPad.
  2. Switch to Design Mode.
  3. As a workaround, a single query is sufficient. To implement the max date logic, we will create a new variable."
  4. Variable Name: Test
  5. Formula: =If(Max(["object name"]) = Max(["object name"]) In (["Primary Key"]); True; False)
  6. Add a filter to the variable object to include only the 'true' records.
  7. Hide the variable column

If you're looking for a permanent solution to this issue, please raise an SAP ticket to request a hotfix.

Conclusion: This blog helps resolve the issue of the " Empty Page Issue of Webi 4.3 PDF Output.

Feedback and comments are appreciated Thanks for stopping by, and I hope this helps at least a few developers/users out there.,

entry.title: Re: Document parameters are not correct from sap bo rest api
entry.content: The way /parameters works is that if all prompts have been answered, then the refresh is executed.  If any prompts remain unanswered, then the response includes the XML like you have in your question.  The reason this is happening is that you need to select a context for each query (you have one context for each of two queries).  Once you do that with another PUT, then you'll get another XML response for the actual prompts.

entry.title: Re: Using BADI IDOC_DATA_INSERT for populating Z fields in VBAK table from IDOC document
entry.content:
Although it's highly unlikely that field of segment types are changed, I recommend NEVER using offsets to address one field:

    lv_valid_from = ls_idoc_data-sdata+286(8).  " Start position 286, length 8
    lv_valid_to   = ls_idoc_data-sdata+294(8).  " End   position 294, length 8

Instead, to make your code easier to understand (that the fields are named CT_VALID_F and CT_VALID_T without any doubt), use:

FIELD-SYMBOLS <e1bpsdhd1> TYPE e1bpsdhd1.

ASSIGN ls_idoc_data-sdata TO <e1bpsdhd1> CASTING.

DATA(lv_valid_from) = <e1bpsdhd1>-ct_valid_f.
DATA(lv_valid_to)   = <e1bpsdhd1>-ct_valid_t.

entry.title: Feedback from the water & wastewater leadership day 2024, Brussels december 5th
entry.content:
Every year, it is becoming a tradition to meet a significant number of water and wastewater services companies interested on innovative strategies and technologies to enhance water resilience and drive digital transformation in their companies. Following the tradition established with the SAP Executive Value Network for Water and Wastewater companies meetings held in Europe and Latin America over the past few years, this year we met in Brussels delegates from 16 companies together with software and system integrators including ESRI, Capgemini, digit’Eaux and SAP.  

 

 

 

The agenda focused on four main topics:  The importance of sector collaboration for innovation, developing a corporate AI strategy, Smart Metering/Smart Water platform, and the adoption of spatial technologies, with inspiring presentations to facilitate comprehensive debates and discussions as related in the following agenda:

  • The importance of sector collaboration:  Flanders collaborative experience for innovation in the Water Sector:  strategy and benefits by De Watergroep
  • Developing an AI strategy in a public company by FARYS
  • Smart Water Platform by Capgemini:
    • Use cases from business perspective
    • Leveraging data intelligence beyond smart metering: Water Balance, energy management and AI driven cases
  • Test and analytics of digital water meters by Suez
  • ESRI on HANA DB:  advantages and possibilities combining data from S/4HANA by ESRI
  • Leveraging spatial data to build strategic asset management by SAP

SAP Keynote by Alain Maus and Miquel Carbó

The first keynote and subject for discussion has been provided by Bert de Winter from De Watergroep who shared his company's experience of collaboration for innovation in the Water Sector among the water companies in the Flemish region, describing the strategy and benefits obtained. It was a very well received presentation, as this approach has proven to deliver significant benefits, such as economies of scale, which cannot easily be achieved by individual approaches. It is also the foundation of the Smart Water Platform, an ongoing collaborative project among De Watergroep in cooperation with FARYS and PIDPA water companies to achieve business outcomes identified around the usage of smart meter data.

Mrs Inge Opreel, CIO of FARYS changed the dynamic of the group discussion by presenting the concepts and steps taken to define a strategy for the adoption, integration, and responsible use of AI in her company.  This presentation generated lots of interest and questions among peer attendees, as they are currently facing the need to evolve from “POCs” – Proof of Concept deliverables – into a much more consistent policy to leverage Artificial Intelligence offerings to improve and achieve gains in operational efficiency, customer engagement, sustainability and other key areas of the company. The discussion also included a discussion of the many challenges associated with AI as well as the need for clarification and common understanding of the term AI and what areas it covers as well as how to explain it. Regarding these mentioned points, a very interesting suggestion by FARYs is to create a “Decision Framework” where to evaluate for each potential use case the adoption criteria can be evaluated based on difference between local and internet connected AI/LLM’s as well as decisions on Sensitivity/privacy of data, ownership of data, need for actual information, need for adaptation, latency, reliability and TCO.

Kevin De Wilde, Engagement Manager and Thought Leader at Capgemini presented a series of use cases of the Smart Water Platform to showcase the value provided by this implementation with the aim of digitizing water networks to address sustainability challenges. After an introductory view of its functional architecture and characteristics, presented some cases of anomalies detected among the installed base of smart meters:  continuous consumption, Backflow / no check valve and complex reports and analysis like the Network water remote quality monitoring and the Network water flow monitoring (water balance, leak detection, ILI, …)

Inge Opreel (FARYS), Bert de Winter (De Watergroep) Kevin de Wilde (Capgemini)

Roseline Klein, from SUEZ Digital Solutions introduced the audience to the Technical Centre for Metering and Measurement (CTCM - Centre Technique Comptage et Mesure) which is responsible for defining the rules for managing the metering and measuring instruments used in drinking water and wastewater in SUEZ operations, but which is also increasingly providing expert support to all players in the metering and instrumentation sector, both in France and abroad, with regards to any problems they may encounter.  Since its creation in 1999, the CTCM works closely with numerous external parties to support R&D efforts and improvement of metering resulting >5.00 meters and instruments controlled each year.  Metering performance and analysis:  regulatory controls and EMP (Weighted Average Error), feedback consolidation and anomaly detection, dispute handling, monitoring and understanding of consumption trends and control and monitoring of strategic meters.

The spatial part of the discussion was inspired by the outstanding presentation of Jeroen van Winden from ESRI. He talked about the multiple levels of integration between ArcGIS and SAP including HANA DB, including some interesting use cases and examples, showing as well cases of integration using API's on web services and at application level in both platforms. This slot was complemented by Miquel Carbó from SAP addressing in his presentation how to leverage spatial data to build strategic asset management, this is a wholistic view of the entire lifecycle of the technical assets, both from the execution part (Investment to Comission or Inspections and corrective maintenance) or in the proactive part (asset risk analysis, health indexes or asset investment planning scenarios) where in all these capabilities the spatial dimension is fundamental.

Jeroen Van Winden (ESRI), Roseline Klein (Suez Digital Solutions), Miquel Carbó (SAP)

Overall, the session was intense and the networking opportunies were very high. The collaboration among SAP and the participant companies has once again raised the expectations of the delegates from the Water and Waste Water industry by providing such high quality inspiring speeches and facilitating fluent and interesting networking to a level that makes it very difficult but a motivating challenge to keep the level for next year!

entry.title: Selling Energy Using the Sales Order Integration into Utilities Sales Contracts
entry.content:
Utilities Retailers moving to SAP S/4HANA Utilities can benefit from a new concept by using the SAP S/4HANA sales orders for selling commodity and non-commodity products. This brings many benefits for the integration of different sales channels. Products, prices and sales processes for commodity and non-commodity products can be better managed and out-of-the-box integration is offered to several SAP solutions.

The target of this blog is to describe this new concept, its benefits, how product definition looks like, and which customizing is required.

Basic concept:

Product Definition:

  • Product Managers can easily create new utilities products by copying template reference products and adding customer-related product information (like contract duration, withdrawal periods, renewable energy, target groups, eligibility).
  • Technically, the utilities products are defined as configurable materials and matched to S/4H Utilities Billing Master Data (rate categories, prices, discounts etc.) via the Utilities Product Integration Layer (UPIL). Information required in Utilities like the contract start date, Meter ID or a termination date are added as characteristics via classes to the product using advanced variant configuration.

Order Management:

  • With the sales integration interface, S/4HANA sales order (formerly known as “SD Order”) are used to capture commodity orders for energy, water, gas, or district heating products. Utilities products can be sold together with physical products like wall boxes, heat pumps or smart home devices. Easy product bundling is also supported. Additional contract lifecycle events, such as product change, or contract termination result in creation of a sales order with the desired product(s) as items. The product configurable attributes, such as meter id or point of delivery can be maintained during order creation.
  • Once the SD order is created and saved, further downstream processes are triggered via the Customer Engagement Process Framework. This process framework orchestrates downstream processing, such as initiating the start of supply process via Market Communication. Market Communication process is responsible for communicating to other market participants and will determine whether the contract creation request/date is valid for the current point of delivery.  Upon successful reply from market communication process, utilities technical master data is created, such as premise, installation, which in turn enables the creation of a Utilities sales contract and Utilities contract (IS-U contract).

Diagram: Order-to-cash process

  Screenshot: Sales order creation for a new contract: overview screen

 Screenshot: Sales order creation for a new contract: configuration of an energy item

Benefits:

  1. Streamline commodity order integration: Use the widely used industry-agnostic SAP S/4HANA sales orders as a unified order integration interface for both commodity and non-commodity products, leveraging the vastly available SD knowledge and talent pool to achieve your integration goals.
  2. Many SAP solutions offer standard out-of-the-box integrations into SAP S/4HANA sales orders which are now relevant for Utilities as well. This reduces the implementation effort and offers new customer facing solutions. Examples are:
    • SAP CPQ to apply pricing and generate quotes (SAP BTP based).
    • SAP Advanced Solution Bundling for easy definition of sales bundles (SAP BTP based).
    • SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing to configure and price your products accurately (SAP BTP based). This cloud service can be integrated and embedded into customer facing solutions reducing integration costs with S/4HANA Utilities significantly.
    • SAP S/4HANA Solution Quotation Management for combinations of different types of products (such as commodity products, tangible products, services, and service contract items). These combinations of products can also be modelled as product bundles in your master data. 
  3. Combine in one order commodity products of different divisions like energy, gas and water. 
  4. Enter the market of solution business and sell commodity products in combination with non-commodity products:
    • for physical products via the SAP S/4HANA sales order,
    • for using full functionality of SAP S/4HANA service contracts and orders or subscriptions the S/4HANA solution quotation can be used to capture and manage mixed orders and create in a second step automatically the sales order, service contracts etc.
  5. Avoid creation of unnecessary master data before deregulated start of supply process is completed successfully.
    • In deregulated markets, the SAP S/4HANA sales order can capture the order and the Customer Engagement Process Framework triggers the start of supply process in SAP Application Process Engine (APE) without creating any technical or commercial master data (beyond the business partner) first.
    • These data are only created after successful completion of the market communication process, thus massively reducing the risk of prematurely creating erroneous or unwanted master data.
  6. Product Managers can easily create new products by copying template reference products and adding additional customer related product information.
    • The reference product (also known as UPIL product) ensures via the Utilities Product Integration Layer (UPIL) the integration to the IS-U billing. The reference product will be set up as a template for a certain product group in advance by a billing master data and UPIL specialist.
  7. Via the creation of different Sales Orders contract lifecycle events and processes for the Utilities sales contract can be triggered.
    • Technically, the Sales Order Item Category indicates which event will be raised. The mapping is defined in customizing. Therefore, it is possible to influence existing contracts upon creation of a sales order. This means the sales order will not be changed, but new sales orders will be created to trigger new lifecycle events.
    • Example: For triggering the termination of a Utilities sales contract, a sales order is created (e.g. via API). The sales order has one item with a product and the item category for “terminate contract". Dedicated characteristics to the "product change" event will be entered as characteristics to the product, e.g. the termination / move out date and the reference ID to the Utilities contract.
    • The following lifecycle events are supported:
      • creation of new Utilities sales contracts,
      • change product from product A to product B,
      • change Utilities sales contract product configuration and prices of the given product,
      • change start date,
      • change end date, 
      • withdraw a Utilities sales contract,
      • terminate a Utilities sales contract, e.g. related to a move-out or customer churn.
  • Screenshot: Sales order item with information on Utilities sales contract termination

8. It is possible to define SAP S/4HANA as the leading system for pricing using the pricing engine and its condition records. Prices can be maintained centrally there and can be replicated to SAP and non-SAP systems.

  • SAP S/4HANA sales has a very powerful and flexible pricing that allows to maintain or upload prices. Prices can be maintained e.g. for
    • usage based working prices (per kWh),
    • yearly recuring base prices,
    • percentage based or fixed amount discounts on the working price or recuring price,
    • scale prices that depend e.g. on the consumed kWh.
  • Sales prices are transferred from SAP S/4HANA pricing engine to utilities sales contracts, and billing for SAP for Utilities (IS-U). The billing integration to the utilities contract is performed by the process framework using the Utilities Product Integration Layer (UPIL).
  • Remark: prices representing cost s.a. grid usage or taxes and levies need not necessarily be modelled as S/4HANA condition records but rather in the classic SAP S/4HANA Utilities billing engine. Processes for changing sales and prices and such underlying price components are likely to differ.
  • The integration to the BTP-based SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing service is a major benefit.
  • Remote management of prices and conditions is supported by a rich set of APIs.
  • The handling of regional prices and price zones as required in the German market can be supported e.g. based on the postal code of the supply to addressee. Prices can then be defined for each price zone that combines several postal codes.

Screenshot: Customizing of SAP Utilities Contract Billing -> Product Integration: Mapping product characteristics to operands and condition types

Availability of features:

The first features of the sales integration interface have been delivered with SAP S/4HANA 2023:

  • The objective is to seamlessly integrate sales orders with customer engagement sales contracts, primarily for new contracts. This integration enables configuration and pricing functionalities within sales orders, which are transferred to utilities sales contracts, utilities business add-ins, and billing for SAP for Utilities (IS-U).

SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS1 in February 2024 

  • Integrate the sales orders in SAP S/4HANA with Utilities sales contract management in Customer Engagement to manage the contract lifecycle of the Utilities sales contract.
    • Change product for utilities sales contract via integrated sales orders
    • End utilities sales contract via integrated sales order
  • Automatically determine or create Contract Account
    • With this feature an example BAdI is provided to determine a contract account in the Process Framework of Customer Engagement from bill to party address and payment method and the creation of new contract account if needed (via template).
  • Handover of sale prices to Utilities prices
    • With this feature sale prices are handed over to Utilities prices to achieve pricing consistency between sales order prices and Utilities prices. Automatic adoption of all price changes to Utilities billing is now possible upon creation of a new order.
    • Prices and price changes and discounts on usage based prices or recurring base prices are maintained as condition records in SAP S/4HANA Sales. 
    • CE-Process Framework takes care about the creation of Utilities price keys based on the product information from UPIL.​
    • A selling system shall integrate with SAP S/4HANA to receive the conditions​.
    • Restriction: no amount simulation for time of use or dynamic pricing. 

SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS2 in October 2024

  • Promotion of the following three additional lifecycle events to influence existing contracts upon creation of a sales order: change start date, change end date, and contract withdrawal.
  • Provisioning of rejection reasons in the sales order for error handling

For future features please see SAP Roadmap Explorer: link 1 and link 2

Customizing:

As part of the “SAP Best Practices for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition” the customizing of the "SAP S/4HANA sales order management and processing" related settings is delivered as standard content: “Sales Integration for SAP S/4HANA Utilities (7JO)”,

  • Process Navigator - SAP for Me

A cookbook on how to customize the sales order and the Sales Integration Interface is provided:

  • Complete List of Cookbooks and Guidelines.pdf
  • see under: SAP S/4HANA Utilities for Customer Engagement -> Sales Integration Interface

entry.title: Inquiry About Time Tracking for Inspections in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am currently exploring options for tracking time spent on inspections within SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, specifically related to Quality Management (QM) processes. My goal is to understand the capabilities of the system in terms of collecting and recording labor time for various inspection activities, such as incoming inspections and final control after goods receipt.

Could you please clarify the following:

  • Is there a standard way to capture time for inspections in the S/4HANA Public Cloud?
  • For example, is there a built-in app or functionality for recording time spent on quality inspections directly within the system?

     

  • Can time tracking for inspections be linked to QM processes or be part of the inspection lot in some way?
  • Are there any recommended best practices or configurations for implementing time tracking for inspections in the cloud environment?

If anyone has experience with similar functionalities or can provide insights into how to configure or use time tracking features, I would greatly appreciate your input.

Thank you in advance for your help!

Best regards,

Atanas

entry.title: My Checklist Steps - Overview of Enhancements in SP15 Version
entry.content:
In the My Checklist Steps app, you can manage all not completed checklist steps that are assigned to you. You can also display all already completed checklist steps that are assigned to you.

Overview of Enhancements in SP15 Version

1. New Links Section
In the object page, we’ve introduced a brand-new section dedicated to links. This section conveniently lists all the links configured with the steps, making it easier for you to access and manage related resources.

2. Attachment Section
In line with the CRM Web UI, we've added an Attachment section. You can now upload, rename, and delete files directly within this section, providing a seamless and integrated experience.

3. Enhanced Option Field
The Option field is now enabled with value validation. This means the option values are dynamically populated based on the backend configuration of your steps, ensuring accuracy and consistency.

 

4. Value Format Validations
We've implemented value format validations for the "To do by" field and Option fields. This helps prevent errors by ensuring that only correctly formatted values are accepted, avoiding any potential issues during the saving process.

5. Save Pending Changes Prompt
To prevent loss of data, we've added a prompt that appears when you press the back navigation button while in edit mode. This pop-up allows you to save any pending changes, ensuring that your work is not lost.

6. Continue to Save Pop-Up Message
When opting to save changes in the pop-up message, the worklist page will be loaded with the saved values. This ensures that all your updates are accurately reflected in the worklist.

These functionalities were already available in the CRM web UI page of the checklists, and we're thrilled to now offer them in the UI5 application, providing a consistent and comprehensive user experience across platforms.

entry.title: Re: remove the deletion indicator from a condition contra...
entry.content:
Hi @adityayadav, 

In SAP CCM, once a condition contract is set to "Logically Deleted," it cannot be reopened or reactivated. This status is intended for contracts that are obsolete or no longer relevant. Given the irreversible nature of this action, it's crucial to use the "Logically Deleted" status with caution. Ensure that a contract is truly obsolete before applying this status.

Regards,
Gauthier Victor

entry.title: Which S/4HANA extensibility option is the best for thriving as an SAP partner in the cloud space?
entry.content:
Get more value from S/4HANA with SAP Extensibility options, part 3

In my first article   of this series, I explained why extensibility options are crucial for S/4HANA customers. In the second article , I explained which option I should choose for the different use cases from a customer's point of view. In this last article, I want to focus on existing and new SAP partners who wish to connect their cloud offerings to S/4HANA customers or migrate their existing SAP ECC add-on to ABAP Cloud.

I have worked for almost 30 years in the SAP ERP space and have seen many innovations in SAP software. I started in 1996 when SAP introduced the client-server ERP and RFC libraries. During the turn of the century, SAP adopted the Internet and launched its new dimension portfolio, and third-party integration products were introduced into the market. SAP has stepped into the cloud in the last decade and fully adopted artificial intelligence.

There are a lot of changes at the core of ERP, but the challenges in my job are almost the same. As a developer, enterprise architect, SAP mentor, speaker, teacher, and advisor, I ask myself, ‘How can I add additional value for my customers out of an ERP system?’ And the answer is always the same. Focus on the missing functionality. SAP always delivers best-practice solutions that never fulfill all customers' requirements. This is still an ample opportunity for me, consultancy companies, SAP add-on solution providers, and all other software vendors.

For the last eight years, I have mostly worked for big cloud companies like Blackline, Booking, and Zuora to advise them to connect their cloud portfolio and systems to SAP S/4HANA. The cloud companies' customers are also big SAP customers. In this article, I will share my experience and tell you which S/4HANA extensibility options fit the best cloud vendors that want to thrive as SAP partners.

Challenges for SAP Solution Partners

The question is easy, but the answer is more complex. As I wrote in my previous article, SAP supports several S/4HANA extensibility options, which depend on the S/4HANA releases and versions. So you need to answer the question: Do you want to support the S/4HANA Cloud private version, the S/4HANA Cloud public version, or even support ‘classic’ SAP ECC?  But also, do you want to build a standalone add-on that runs on Business Technology Platform Software as a Service (BTP SAAS) or is embedded on S/4HANA? Or do you want to connect your partner cloud solution to S/4HANA?

But there are more answers needed. The data and events must be publicly available in the S/4HANA system. How will we handle the data exchange's latency, performance, size, and security? Who will be responsible when something goes wrong, technical, infra, and business-wise? Will the current SAP license be enough, or will there be additional operational and license costs for my customers? How will user management, authentication, authorization, and security be handled? How will the execution be monitored, and how will alerting and mapping be arranged? How should sensitive data be managed, and what audit trails and data should be archived? How will networking, security, risk, and complaint departments react to the solution? Will the used platform and tooling be future-proof? And so we can ask a lot more questions.

Ultimately, all these questions should be answered to develop the best solution. However, in my experience, not all SAP partners consider these questions.

SAP positions the S/4HANA Cloud public edition as the preferred version to GROW with SAP in the long term. However, SAP also provides the S/4HANA Cloud private edition for existing customers to RISE, with SAP as the golden path to move to the S/4HANA Cloud public edition in the future.

We will first focus on the S/4HANA Cloud public edition because this release is SAP’s long-term solution. Next, we can consider other SAP ERP systems, such as the S/4HANA Cloud private edition, S/4HANA on-premise, and SAP ECC.

S/4HANA extensibility options

In my second article, I described the eight extensibility options for S/4HANA. However, if we look at partner solutions, we can skip the classic extensibilities because they are not supported in the public edition and are obsolete from a certification point of view. Also, the low-code extensibilities options S/4HANA Key User Extensibility, SAP Analytics Cloud, and SAP Build Apps could be more suitable because they mainly focus on custom developments for the citizen developer.

Figure 1 – Possible Partner Integration Options

 

This limited the possible extensibility options, and because we can also access the S/4HANA APIs and events directly, we will have five possibilities (See Figure 1) to deliver a partner solution:

  • An add-on application on the S/4HANA embedded ABAP environment
  • A partner application as a SAAS application running on SAP BTP ABAP environment
  • A partner application as a SAAS application developed with SAP Build Code on SAP BTP
  • An integration package on SAP Cloud Integration
  • Running your application on a non-SAP solution and directly accessing S/4HANA events and APIs.

Using APIs and events

Software vendors that run their solutions on their cloud will primarily consider the last option and try to connect their cloud solution directly to S/4HANA events and APIs. But from my experience, this isn’t the right approach. The S/4HANA Cloud public edition strongly depends on business events, and most cloud vendor solutions aren’t prepared for this. Due to the configuration possibilities in the S/4HANA Cloud and the use of APIs, the responsibility for root cause analysis shifts from the customer to the cloud vendor, which needs to build a big SAP knowledge support center. Also, the cloud vendor should handle all security, risk, and compliance issues, which are initially the customer's responsibility.

Another issue with this approach is that you, as a partner, cannot certify the solution with SAP. SAP is not responsible for the solution's availability and performance, code changes, or deprecation of APIs and events. This approach carries a high risk for customers and the cloud vendor.

SAP Cloud Integration

A better approach is an integration package that can be installed on the customer's SAP Cloud Integration system. In this case, SAP will ensure smooth integration between S/4HANA and SAP Cloud Integration. The solution can be certified, and as a cloud vendor, you can mainly focus on integrating your cloud partner solution and SAP Cloud integration. The downside of this approach is that SAP Cloud Integration is separate from any S/4HANA Cloud edition license and should be brought by the customer separately. Only some customers are willing to do so. Most customers already have an existing integration product for more than just their SAP landscape. The need for separate SAP Cloud Integration licenses and setting up a support and knowledge center to maintain SAP Cloud Integration will be a blocker. Providing an integration package as a partner solution will limit the number of SAP customers you can connect to your cloud solution in the market.

ABAP Cloud environments

The best approach for cloud vendors is to build the connectivity as an add-on product on S/4HANA, a SAAS solution on BTP, or a combination of these options. Building your partner integration solution on an ABAP Cloud environment is the best extensibility option for applications strongly dependent on S/4HANA. Based on my investigation, cloud vendors will profit the most from this option. They can shift the responsibility of the availability and maintenance of the landscape to SAP; they are highly flexible in a robust and stable environment following SAP’s future path and can directly profit from the innovations SAP is launching. The barriers to developing these extensions are higher, but the barriers for customers will be lower. There will be no additional costs for running the developer extensions on the embedded ABAP cloud environment in S/4HANA Cloud. When cloud vendors run their solution as a SAAS solution on BTP, they can share the costs with multiple customers. The advantage of the SAAS solution above the Addon is that the cloud vendor fully controls the code line, and the customer is still responsible for accessing and connecting to their S/4HANA tenant.

Another significant advantage of building connectivity from S/4HANA to your cloud solution on ABAP Cloud for BTP as a SAAS solution is that you can connect to the S/4HANA cloud public edition, make your future-proof solution with one code line, and downport the connection to other ERP versions, such as the S/4HANA cloud private edition, S/4HANA on-premise, or even SAP ECC.

Figure 2 – Codeline options of ABAP Cloud

 

Because the ABAP Cloud on the BTP code line follows SAP’s Clean core strategy, it should also run as an add-on on the S/4HANA Cloud editions when these editions support the same underlying ABAP Cloud version.

As mentioned before, in SAP ECC, many SAP solution partners have already provided an add-on to solve the missing functionality of standard SAP. However, this add-on should be rewritten to be S/4HANA Cloud compliant because SAP only accepts code that follows SAP’s Clean core strategy. The SAP solution partner can rewrite the code as S/4HANA Cloud Developer Extensibility, but in this case, the partner always depends on the customers' upgrade strategy. Suppose he wants to control the versioning of his code line and innovations. In that case, the partner can rewrite the addon as a side-by-side extensibility with SAP Build Code in SAP CAP or ABAP, which connects to S/4HANA. Or he can even run the applications in a non-SAP cloud environment of his choice and follow the extensibility options described above. From my experience, all these options are valid, future-proof, and in line with the solution for cloud vendors.

Cloud and SAP Add-on vendors who want to build their solutions with ABAP Cloud can even speed up their development, shorten the time to market, and shift responsibilities back to their customers using an OEM solution like the FEXS API Connect from fexs.com. These OEM solutions already take care of the most common tasks, resulting in less development needed, smaller SAP support departments, and higher customer satisfaction.

Conclusion

Because SAP and its customers are still on the move with S/4HANA to its future Cloud ERP landscape, it isn’t easy for SAP Solution partners to make the right decision for their future-proof solution.  The move of SAP to the cloud and the openness and flexibility of its portfolio will give SAP Addon partners and existing cloud solution providers an excellent opportunity to bring added value to the existing SAP community.  Suppose your solution strongly depends on S/4HANA processes. In that case, I believe a partner application running as S/4HANA Cloud Developer Extensibility or on a SAP BTP ABAP Environment following SAP Clean Core strategy is the best and most future-proof solution. An article is always too short to explain all the details, but I hope this article triggers your mind.  And in my job as a strategic architect and advisor, I’m open to any discussion and available to help push you in the right direction.

This article was initially posted on the linked-in profile of Robert Eijpe

entry.title: Re: How to manage table and report password fields
entry.content:
How secure do you want your password to be encrypted?

Based on this answer, check the SAP Abap tools available for this, or even external tools.

  • Encrypt it, then store it in the database
  • Also check that some input field respects this password (Is it possible not to explicitly decrypt the password - prevent debug to create a security hole...)

What did you already look for, try to use?

entry.title: Message no. M7242 - Valuation control in material master and special stock differs
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

An issue arises in the 'Enter Inspection Results from Warehouse' app in Public Cloud (BEST PRACTICE - BKP - Customer returns).

After processing a return delivery, I selected the 'replacement order' (free-of-charge delivery is created with reference to replacement order) option as a follow-up action. This triggers an automatic requirement for a free-of-charge delivery. However, when I proceed with production and attempt to do the final confirmation and receive the goods, the following error appears and prevents the goods receipt against the production order.

Valuation control in material master and special stock differs

 

Message no. M7242

 

Diagnosis

 

You want to post a material document in which the special stock
indicator is different from the entries in the material master record.
This can lead to data inconsistencies, so the system does not permit
this posting.

Could you please provide a solution or guidance on how to resolve these issues? Thank you very much!

Best regards,
Kaushika

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
